brief_justification: Paper A is a thermodynamic lifecycle-accounting benchmark (EROI ratios and GWP100 carbon intensities recomputed as the system boundary is expanded to include grids, storage, and firming), whereas paper B is a decision-theoretic decomposition of a subjective risk under a strictly proper loss into epistemic and aleatoric information-theoretic terms; the only overlap is the generic word 'decomposition/boundary expansion' with no shared governing equation, estimation principle, or role-for-role correspondence between energy-return ratios and proper-scoring-rule uncertainty terms.
